nodes.x: Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,
Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
Furthermore Elihu answered and said,
So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the Lord commanded them: the Lord also accepted Job.
Then Eli answered and said, Go in peace: and the God of Israel grant thee thy petition that thou hast asked of him.
Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?
Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.
The lips of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish doeth not so.
Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom.
And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.
Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?
Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?
Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.

And so with the man who will think and , say something when he * talks/ At first, because of the restriction he puts on himself, he can’t * talk’ so freely as he did; and nobody will regret that! But leb him persevere in ‘ talking* no faster than he can think , and in process of time he will speak as freely as before, and say something good all the time he * talks.’ ”
Again, verily I say unto you that he can also lift up his voice in meetings, whenever it shall be expedient.
When he giveth quietness, who then can make trouble? and when he hideth his face, who then can behold him? whether it be done against a nation, or against a man only:
Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God.
But as for thee and thy servants, I know that ye will not yet fear the Lord God.
Abide thou with me, fear not: for he that seeketh my life seeketh thy life: but with me thou shalt be in safeguard.
But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
But I will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.

“Fear,” said he, “the Lord, and keep His 
commandments.’ For if you keep the com- 
mandments of God, you will be powerful in 
every action, and every one of your actions will 
be incomparable. For, fearing the Lord, you 
will do all things well. This is the fear which 
you ought to have, that you may be saved. But 
fear not the devil; for, fearing the Lord, you 
will have dominion over the devil,…
For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.
Thou lovest all devouring words, O thou deceitful tongue.
Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
¶ When thou comest into thy neighbour’s vineyard, then thou mayest eat grapes thy fill at thine own pleasure; but thou shalt not put any in thy vessel.
Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.
But the word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.
Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.
Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.
My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.

And again, verily I say unto you, if after thine enemy has come upon thee the first time, he repent and come unto thee praying thy forgiveness, thou shalt forgive him, and shalt hold it no more as a testimony against thine enemy—
And he laid it upon my mouth, and said: Lo, this has touched thy lips; and thine iniquity is taken away, and thy sin purged.
Art thou the first man that was born? or wast thou made before the hills?
Knowest thou it, because thou wast then born? or because the number of thy days is great?
The first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the Lord thy God. Thou shalt not seethe a kid in his mother’s milk.
And Isaac his father said unto him, Who art thou? And he said, I am thy son, thy firstborn Esau.
The Son of Man hath descended below them all. Art thou greater than he?
And thou art after the order of him who was without beginning of days or end of years, from all eternity to all eternity.
Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?
But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret; and thy Father, who seeth in secret, shall reward thee openly.
Behold, thou desirest truth in the inward parts: and in the hidden part thou shalt make me to know wisdom.
But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut thy door, pray to thy Father which is in secret; and thy Father which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly.
How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
Yea, and thou heardest not; yea, thou knewest not; yea, from that time thine ear was not opened; for I knew that thou wouldst deal very treacherously, and wast called a transgressor from the womb.
What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?
¶ And the people said unto me, Wilt thou not tell us what these things are to us, that thou doest so?
Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.

If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider it? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth not he know it? and shall not he render to every man according to his works?
Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.
Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.
With us are both the grayheaded and very aged men, much elder than thy father.
The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the gray head.
Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.
Rebuke not an elder, but entreat him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;
And the firstborn said unto the younger, Our father is old, and there is not a man in the earth to come in unto us after the manner of all the earth:
Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers?
Are the consolations of God small with thee? is there any secret thing with thee?
I have even heard of thee, that the spirit of the gods is in thee, and that light and understanding and excellent wisdom is found in thee.

And the Levite, (because he hath no part nor inheritance with thee,) and the stranger, and the fatherless, and the widow, which are within thy gates, shall come, and shall eat and be satisfied; that the Lord thy God may bless thee in all the work of thine hand which thou doest.
And these things hast thou hid in thine heart: I know that this is with thee.
If there be therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels and mercies,

Yea, he that repenteth and exerciseth faith, and bringeth forth good works, and prayeth continually without ceasing—unto such it is given to know the mysteries of God; yea, unto such it shall be given to reveal things which never have been revealed; yea, and it shall be given unto such to bring thousands of souls to repentance, even as it has been given unto us to bring these our brethren to…
Why doth thine heart carry thee away? and what do thy eyes wink at,
He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.
The eye of him that hath seen me shall see me no more: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.
So that thou shalt be mad for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
That thine eyes may be open unto the supplication of thy servant, and unto the supplication of thy people Israel, to hearken unto them in all that they call for unto thee.
That thou turnest thy spirit against God, and lettest such words go out of thy mouth?
Also take no heed unto all words that are spoken; lest thou hear thy servant curse thee:
Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
Thy word have I hid in mine heart, that I might not sin against thee.
What is man, that he should be clean? and he which is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?
How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?
And the priest that maketh him clean shall present the man that is to be made clean, and those things, before the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:
Wherewithal shall a young man cleanse his way? by taking heed thereto according to thy word.
But no man is possessor of all things except he be purified and cleansed from all sin.

But the man that is clean, and is not in a journey, and forbeareth to keep the passover, even the same soul shall be cut off from among his people: because he brought not the offering of the Lord in his appointed season, that man shall bear his sin.
Behold, he putteth no trust in his saints; yea, the heavens are not clean in his sight.
He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.
How much more abominable and filthy is man, which drinketh iniquity like water?
What man is like Job, who drinketh up scorning like water?
And whomsoever he toucheth that hath the issue, and hath not rinsed his hands in water, he shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.
I will shew thee, hear me; and that which I have seen I will declare;
Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
I will praise thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
For in thee, O Lord, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
And I will shew mercies unto you, that he may have mercy upon you, and cause you to return to your own land.
Which wise men have told from their fathers, and have not hid it:
That wise men and rulers may hear and know that which they have never considered;
The wise men are ashamed, they are dismayed and taken: lo, they have rejected the word of the Lord; and what wisdom is in them?
For there is no remembrance of the wise more than of the fool for ever; seeing that which now is in the days to come shall all be forgotten. And how dieth the wise man? as the fool.
Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a fool, that he may be wise.
Unto whom alone the earth was given, and no stranger passed among them.

And all the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing: and he doeth according to his will in the army of heaven, and among the inhabitants of the earth: and none can stay his hand, or say unto him, What doest thou?
Therefore they separated themselves one from another, and went forth among them, every man alone, according to the word and power of God which was given unto him.
And it came to pass, when all the people were clean passed over, that the ark of the Lord passed over, and the priests, in the presence of the people.
And it came to pass that I saw the multitudes of the earth gathered together.
The wicked man travaileth with pain all his days, and the number of years is hidden to the oppressor.
The fear of the Lord prolongeth days: but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.
Lord, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked triumph?

with him, and the wicked will be destroyed from off the face of the earth. I meant to be short this afternoon; but really, when I get to studying on these things, I forget myself, and oftentimes weary the patience of the people. God bless you! Amen.
For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
A dreadful sound is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.
A noise shall come even to the ends of the earth; for the Lord hath a controversy with the nations, he will plead with all flesh; he will give them that are wicked to the sword, saith the Lord.
He delivereth the poor in his affliction, and openeth their ears in oppression.
Wealth and riches shall be in his house: and his righteousness endureth for ever.
He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.
In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him.
He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.
He mocketh at fear, and is not affrighted; neither turneth he back from the sword.
He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.
He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.
He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.
Thou hast also turned the edge of his sword, and hast not made him to stand in the battle.
He wandereth abroad for bread, saying, Where is it? he knoweth that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.
And he shall be as the light of the morning, when the sun riseth, even a morning without clouds; as the tender grass springing out of the earth by clear shining after rain.
And he said unto his daughters, And where is he? why is it that ye have left the man? call him, that he may eat bread.
And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
¶ Now the first day of the feast of unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover?
His breath goeth forth, he returneth to his earth; in that very day his thoughts perish.
Trouble and anguish shall make him afraid; they shall prevail against him, as a king ready to the battle.
And I will come upon him while he is weary and weak handed, and will make him afraid: and all the people that are with him shall flee; and I will smite the king only:
For when the Lord shall appear he shall be terrible unto them, that fear may seize upon them, and they shall stand afar off and tremble.
And he shall pass over to his strong hold for fear, and his princes shall be afraid of the ensign, saith the Lord, whose fire is in Zion, and his furnace in Jerusalem.
His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.
The fear of a king is as the roaring of a lion: whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.
For he stretcheth out his hand against God, and strengtheneth himself against the Almighty.
By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.
For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth his hands among us, and multiplieth his words against God.
And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.
He runneth upon him, even on his neck, upon the thick bosses of his bucklers:
Thy neck is like the tower of David builded for an armoury, whereon there hang a thousand bucklers, all shields of mighty men.
He sendeth forth his commandment upon earth: his word runneth very swiftly.
His enemies will I clothe with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish.
He hath also prepared for him the instruments of death; he ordaineth his arrows against the persecutors.
Hast thou given the horse strength? hast thou clothed his neck with thunder?
Because he covereth his face with his fatness, and maketh collops of fat on his flanks.
He that is of a proud heart stirreth up strife: but he that putteth his trust in the Lord shall be made fat.
Because they provoked his spirit, so that he spake unadvisedly with his lips.
And he took the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that was upon the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and the right shoulder:
And he shall offer of it all the fat thereof; the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards,
And he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
And he dwelleth in desolate cities, and in houses which no man inhabiteth, which are ready to become heaps.
For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his bishoprick let another take.
And there he maketh the hungry to dwell, that they may prepare a city for habitation;
He that is so impoverished that he hath no oblation chooseth a tree that will not rot; he seeketh unto him a cunning workman to prepare a graven image, that shall not be moved.
And he knew their desolate palaces, and he laid waste their cities; and the land was desolate, and the fulness thereof, by the noise of his roaring.
Then said I: Lord, how long? And he said: Until the cities be wasted without inhabitant, and the houses without man, and the land be utterly desolate;
He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall he prolong the perfection thereof upon the earth.
So is he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.
He that hasteth to be rich hath an evil eye, and considereth not that poverty shall come upon him.
I say unto you, wo be unto that man, for his substance shall perish with him; and now, I say these things unto those who are rich as pertaining to the things of this world.
He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.
All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him.
He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.
His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.
Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.
Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.
Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.
The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.
With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.
Blessed is that man that maketh the Lord his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.

There is a vanity which is done upon the earth; that there be just men, unto whom it happeneth according to the work of the wicked; again, there be wicked men, to whom it happeneth according to the work of the righteous: I said that this also is vanity.
It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green.
He is green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.

For he shall be as a tree planted by the waters, and that spreadeth out her roots by the river, and shall not see when heat cometh, but her leaf shall be green; and shall not be careful in the year of drought, neither shall cease from yielding fruit.
And shall consume the glory of his forest, and of his fruitful field, both soul and body; and they shall be as when a standard-bearer fainteth.
He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive.

¶ Yet gleaning grapes shall be left in it, as the shaking of an olive tree, two or three berries in the top of the uppermost bough, four or five in the outmost fruitful branches thereof, saith the Lord God of Israel.
¶ When thus it shall be in the midst of the land among the people, there shall be as the shaking of an olive tree, and as the gleaning grapes when the vintage is done.

And he fenced it, and gathered out the stones thereof, and planted it with the choicest vine, and built a tower in the midst of it, and also made a wine-press therein; and he looked that it should bring forth grapes, and it brought forth wild grapes.

And it came to pass that the master of the vineyard went forth, and he saw that his olive tree began to decay; and he said: I will prune it, and dig about it, and nourish it, that perhaps it may shoot forth young and tender branches, and it perish not.
Binding his foal unto the vine, and his ass’s colt unto the choice vine; he washed his garments in wine, and his clothes in the blood of grapes:
For the congregation of hypocrites shall be desolate, and fire shall consume the tabernacles of bribery.

Yet they shall be ministers in my sanctuary, having charge at the gates of the house, and ministering to the house: they shall slay the burnt offering and the sacrifice for the people, and they shall stand before them to minister unto them.
And they shall keep his charge, and the charge of the whole congregation before the tabernacle of the congregation, to do the service of the tabernacle.
And they shall be joined unto thee, and keep the charge of the tabernacle of the congregation, for all the service of the tabernacle: and a stranger shall not come nigh unto you.
And they shall keep all the instruments of the tabernacle of the congregation, and the charge of the children of Israel, to do the service of the tabernacle.

And he shall make an atonement for the holy sanctuary, and he shall make an atonement for the tabernacle of the congregation, and for the altar, and he shall make an atonement for the priests, and for all the people of the congregation.
They conceive mischief, and bring forth vanity, and their belly prepareth deceit.
For when they speak great swelling words of vanity, they allure through the lusts of the flesh, through much wantonness, those that were clean escaped from them who live in error.
For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.
They draw nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.
And being thus prepared they supposed that they should easily overpower and subject their brethren to the yoke of bondage, or slay and massacre them according to their pleasure.
They that observe lying vanities forsake their own mercy.
